text.title: Search Everywhere Optimization Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Search Everywhere Optimization application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Plan MUST name ≥3 target platforms with rationale per platform. Google-only plans are rejected.
rule.rationale: Search demand has fragmented across Google + AI assistants + video; single-platform plans miss most of the surface area.

rule.statement: Each piece of content MUST use AI-extractable structure (headings + lists + Q&A + schema). Wall-of-text content is rejected.
rule.rationale: Generative engines prefer chunked content; unstructured content rarely gets cited.

rule.statement: Plan MUST identify the citation moment (which question, which platform). Generic 'visibility' goals are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a citation target the work cannot be measured or optimised.

rule.statement: Each content piece MUST carry author bio + credentials + last-updated date. Anonymous content is rejected.
rule.rationale: Both Google E-E-A-T and AI engines weight authorship; anonymous content is ranked down across the board.

rule.statement: Plan MUST include a monthly citation-monitoring task (Perplexity / ChatGPT / Google AI Overviews). Untracked plans are rejected.
rule.rationale: Citation gains are invisible without monitoring; the loop cannot close.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
